expectations: The agent selects design-review and runs the surface critique path as a target comparison.
The agent does not route to visual-design or a code-review skill.

task.prompt: Here's a screenshot of the dashboard I just built and the Figma mock it was based on — check whether the implementation matches the design.

expectations: The agent selects design-review's surface critique path.
The output ends in a pass/block gate decision.

task.prompt: Before I hand this landing page off, do a design self-review of the rendered page.

expectations: The agent selects design-review's flow audit path.
The output includes a numbered step list with health labels.

task.prompt: Audit our onboarding flow from signup through creating a first project — tell me where users will struggle.

expectations: The agent selects design-review's flow audit path in combined mode.
Accessibility claims from screenshots alone are labeled likely or needs-testing.

task.prompt: Go through the checkout experience on our staging site and critique it — UX and accessibility.

expectations: The agent does not select design-review; code review belongs to a code-review skill such as ultra-review.

task.prompt: Review this diff — I changed the dashboard UI components and want to make sure the code is solid.

expectations: The agent does not select design-review; redesign and polish belong to visual-design.
No audit or critique report is produced instead of design work.

task.prompt: This settings page feels cluttered — redesign it so it breathes more.

expectations: The agent routes to visual-design (build/polish), not design-review.
Critique appears only as part of the design workflow, not as the deliverable.

task.prompt: Take a look at this app and make it look more professional.

expectations: Reasonable outcomes include design-review's surface critique or a clarifying question; a full flow audit or an unrequested redesign is a miss.
Mark for human review when the routing is unclear.
